Colch..Chest: Stinging, tearing pains in the muscles of the chest.
Colch..Heart & Circulation, palpitation: Palpitation; oppression of the chest. Feels as if he had a great weight on the chest; cannot breathe.
Colch..Chill: He is a chilly patient, sensitive to cold.
Colch..Extremities: Numbness, oedema, swelling of the limbs.
Colch..Extremities, paralysis: Paralysis of the limbs or of one limb, or of any part.
Colch..Generalities: All the complaints of this remedy are aggravated from motion. The painful complaints, the head complaints, the bowel complaints. the liver complaints, the stomach complaints, are all worse from motion.
Colch..Generalities, weakness, enervation: Great weakness and exhaustion. He cannot exert himself in the least without causing dyspnea, Must lie down; does not want to move; sinking of strength; seems as if his life will flow out of him from motion and from exertion; so tired and exhausted.
Colch..Head: The headaches are all of a rheumatic character. Very often the whole skull, the pericranium, is sore as if bruised. The scalp is sensitive. Pressure in the head- constriction; pressing, bursting headaches. Heat in the head. Tearing in the scalp. Headaches are all aggravated by motion.
Colch..Mind: Colchicum has delirium, prostration, depression of mind, great sensitiveness to pain, which he seems to feel in his mind, and it brings out mental symptoms. Very sensitive to pain; confusion of the mind; disorders of comprehension. Cannot understand what he reads.
Colch..Rectum: Great relaxation of the parts. Protrusion of the rectum.
Colch..Rectum, pain: Very painful, extremely painful is the Colchicum stool. Great soreness in the abdomen.
Colch..Stomach: Nausea, gagging, retching at the bare mention of food in his presence. The thought and smell of food bring on nausea and vomiting.
Colch..Appetite, aversion, meat: The smell of fish, eggs, fat meats or broths causes nausea even unto faintness.
Colch..Stomach, distension: The abdomen is distended with flatus, tympanitic. Great soreness in the whole abdomen just such a tympanitic condition as we have in typhoid.
Colch..Stomach, pain: In the stomach there is sometimes coldness and sometimes burning.
Colch..Stomach, pain, burning: Burning in the pit of the stomach.
Colch..Stomach, thirst: The Colchicum patient may have much thirst or no thirst, or these may alternate.
Colch..Abdomen, pain: Spasmodic pains, colic, tearing pains, burning, griping pains, forcing the patient to bend double. Aggravated from motion. Great tenderness and soreness with the colic. Aggravated from eating; ameliorated from bending double.
Colch..Generalities, faintness: The smell of fish, eggs, fat meats or broths causes nausea even unto faintness.
Colch..Generalities, pain: Touch and motion bring on a painful sensation in the body as of electric vibrations.
Colch..Clinical, paralysis: A paralytic weakness was observed in the poisonous effects and too prolonged provings. The jaw hangs down, the muscles are flabby, relaxed. Paralysis of the limbs or of one limb, or of any part.
Colch..Generalities, weakness: Great prostration accompanies the complaints of this remedy. Weakness of the limbs, great exhaustion, nervous exhaustion of a typhoid character. He gradually grows weaker like one going into Bright's disease.
Colch..Appetite: Absolutely unable to touch food. Nausea, gagging, retching at the bare mention of food in his presence.
Colch..Appetite, aversion: Aversion to food; loathing the sight and smell, more the smell of it.
Colch..Clinical: Rheumatic troubles that have been going on for some time, will end in cardiac troubles. When cardiac troubles with valvular defects are present, almost the first thing the busy doctor thinks of is a history of rheumatism.
Colch..Heart & Circulation, heart: Heart's action muffled, indistinct, very weak.
